task_completion.description: Verify the agent opens and reads the mission specifications from the requested text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit or nano) or terminal window displaying the contents of 'ephemeris_request.txt' from the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent configures a Moon-centered MJ2000Eq coordinate system in GMAT.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A GMAT Coordinate System configuration dialog showing the 'Origin' set to 'Luna' (or Moon) and 'Axes' set to 'MJ2000Eq'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent sets the propagator step size to 60 seconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A GMAT Propagator configuration dialog (e.g., DefaultProp) showing the 'Step Size' or 'Initial Step Size' field set to 60.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent configures the mission sequence to propagate for exactly 7 days.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A GMAT 'Propagate' command dialog in the Mission tree showing the stopping condition set to 'ElapsedDays' with a value of 7.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent configures the EphemerisFile resources for both OEM and SPK formats with the correct paths.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT EphemerisFile configuration dialogs showing the file format set to 'CCSDS-OEM' with the path '~/GMAT_output/lunar_dsn.oem', and another set to 'SPK' with the path '~/GMAT_output/lunar_science.bsp'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent saves the GMAT script to the correct location and executes the mission to generate the files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GMAT window title or save dialog showing the script saved as 'lunar_dsn.script' in '~/Documents/missions/'. Additionally, evidence of execution such as the GMAT message window showing a successful run, an orbit track rendering, or a file explorer showing the newly generated .oem and .bsp files.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used the GMAT application to configure the mission.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the GMAT graphical user interface being interacted with (e.g., Resource tree, Mission tree, dialog boxes), rather than just writing a script from scratch in a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent generated the ephemeris files via GMAT execution.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the GMAT 'Run' button being pressed or the GMAT output window showing execution progress. The agent should not be manually typing or copying text into a file named 'lunar_dsn.oem'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not attempt to fake the binary SPK file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not open a text editor to manually create 'lunar_science.bsp', as BSP files are binary SPICE kernels that must be generated by software.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The mission specifications are located at /home/ga/Desktop/ephemeris_request.txt. The required coordinate system is Moon-centered MJ2000Eq. The propagator must use a 60-second step size. The mission duration is exactly 7 days. The expected outputs are a CCSDS OEM file at /home/ga/GMAT_output/lunar_dsn.oem, a SPICE SPK file at /home/ga/GMAT_output/lunar_science.bsp, and the GMAT script saved to /home/ga/Documents/missions/lunar_dsn.script. A correct 7-day propagation at 60-second intervals will yield exactly 10,081 data points in the OEM file.
